Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. DOS Memory Management 35 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. The Cathode Ray Tube site 42 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. First in-utero stem cell therapy for fetal spina bifida repair is safe: study 293 upvotes, 51 comments
  4. The 185-Microsecond Type Hint 66 upvotes, 8 comments
  5. Guido van Rossum Interviews Thomas Wouters (Python Core Dev) 23 upvotes, 1 comments
  6. RCade: Building a Community Arcade Cabinet 78 upvotes, 14 comments
  7. Simple screw counter 109 upvotes, 23 comments
  8. Physicists developing a quantum computer that’s entirely open source 94 upvotes, 20 comments
  9. Show HN: I built a sub-500ms latency voice agent from scratch 370 upvotes, 108 comments
  10. Inside the M4 Apple Neural Engine, Part 1: Reverse Engineering 328 upvotes, 95 comments
  11. Seed of Might Color Correction Process (2023) [pdf] 85 upvotes, 21 comments
  12. Motorola announces a partnership with GrapheneOS 2172 upvotes, 798 comments
  13. Launch HN: OctaPulse (YC W26) – Robotics and computer vision for fish farming 99 upvotes, 34 comments
  14. Show HN: Govbase – Follow a bill from source text to news bias to social posts 189 upvotes, 76 comments
  15. Arm's Cortex X925: Reaching Desktop Performance 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  16. Buckle Up for Bumpier Skies 23 upvotes, 5 comments
  17. British Columbia is permanently adopting daylight time 764 upvotes, 383 comments
  18. Meta’s AI smart glasses and data privacy concerns 1008 upvotes, 577 comments
  19. I built a pint-sized Macintosh 17 upvotes, 4 comments
  20. Ars Technica fires reporter after AI controversy involving fabricated quotes 236 upvotes, 139 comments
  21. Against Query Based Compilers 60 upvotes, 33 comments
  22. Programmable Cryptography (2024) 66 upvotes, 39 comments
  23. Daily Driving GrapheneOS 82 upvotes, 54 comments
  24. Elevated Errors in Claude.ai 102 upvotes, 90 comments
  25. Intent-Based Commits 40 upvotes, 31 comments
  26. Moldova broke our data pipeline 39 upvotes, 33 comments
  27. Ask HN: Who is hiring? (March 2026) 207 upvotes, 241 comments
  28. Guilty Displeasures 59 upvotes, 62 comments
  29. iPhone 17e 265 upvotes, 370 comments
  30. New iPad Air, powered by M4 381 upvotes, 604 comments

Last generated 4:30pm, 3 March 2026. Source here.
